title: Heterogeneous Database Migration
description:
## Oracle to Aurora PostgreSQL with Minimal Downtime

A company must migrate a large, busy **on-premises Oracle** database to **Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL**. The application runs 24/7, so the cutover window must be **minimal** — the business will tolerate only a few minutes of downtime. Because the source and target engines differ, the **schema and proprietary objects** (stored procedures, data types) must be converted. Which approach BEST meets these requirements?

options.text: Use the AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) to convert the schema, then AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) with full-load plus ongoing change data capture (CDC) to keep the target in sync until a brief cutover
Use Application Migration Service (MGN) to replicate the database server block-level and cut over to EC2
Take an Oracle data dump (Data Pump export), copy it to S3, and import it into Aurora during a maintenance window
Create an Oracle RDS snapshot and restore it as an Aurora PostgreSQL cluster

explanation:
Oracle → Aurora PostgreSQL is a **heterogeneous** migration (different engines), so you need two things: **schema conversion** and **low-downtime data movement**. The **AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)** converts the schema and flags/rewrites proprietary objects (PL/SQL, data types). **AWS DMS** then performs a **full load** and switches to **ongoing replication via change data capture (CDC)**, applying changes from the live Oracle source to Aurora so the target stays current until a brief, planned cutover — meeting the minimal-downtime requirement.

(b) **MGN** rehosts *servers* block-level to EC2; it does not convert a database engine or migrate between Oracle and PostgreSQL — wrong tool for a DB engine change. (c) A **Data Pump dump → S3 → import** is a one-time bulk load with significant downtime (no continuous sync), and Data Pump output is Oracle-specific — it won't load into PostgreSQL without conversion. (d) **Snapshot/restore across engines is impossible**: an Oracle RDS snapshot cannot be restored as Aurora PostgreSQL; snapshots only restore to the same engine. SCT + DMS (with CDC) is the canonical heterogeneous, minimal-downtime pattern.

hints.title: Same engine or different?
hints.body: Different source/target engines means you need schema conversion (SCT) on top of data migration (DMS).

hints.title: Minimal downtime
hints.body: Ongoing replication (CDC) is what lets you keep the target in sync and cut over in minutes rather than during a long offline load.
